title: Medical device shortages: Report the end of a shortage - Canada.ca
content:
## Medical device shortages: Report the end of a shortage
- List of specified medical devices
- List of shortages and discontinuations
- Report the end of a shortage
- Report a discontinuation
Manufacturers of Class I to IV medical devices and importers of Class I medical devices must report the end of a medical device shortage for devices that are on the List of Medical Devices - Notification of Shortages . Submit your report 2 business days after meeting the demand for the medical device.
The requirements for reporting the end of a shortage are outlined in section 62.23(2) of the Medical Devices Regulations .
To support medical device shortage and discontinuation  reporting, Health Canada has posted Guidance for reporting medical device  shortages and discontinuations (GUI-0137) .
Report the end of a shortage
Include the following information when reporting the end of a medical device shortage:
- name of the device and of any component, accessory, or part of the affected device, including the model name, if applicable
- device identifier includes the identifier of any medical device that is part of a system, test kit, medical device group, medical device family or device group family
- includes the identifier of any medical device that is part of a system, test kit, medical device group, medical device family or device group family
- name and contact information for the manufacturer or the importer
- rationale for why the shortage has ended
- date of the end of the shortage
Complete these fields when submitting a report to  notify us of the end of a shortage.
